column1: GPS JITTER BASELINE  --  STAGE A OF PROMISE MAKER GPS AUDIT
Cohort          : Pan-India mobiles emitting wifi_connected_location_captured in this window
Status          : Stage A complete. Stage B (booking-vs-install drift) next.
Promise Maker gates every lead on a 25m serviceability test. That test consumes
the booking-time lat/lng. Before we can claim 'the booking coordinate is clean/dirty',
we have to know the intrinsic noise floor of the GPS apparatus itself.
Stage A (this folder) establishes that floor using repeat SSID-validated pings.
Stage B will compare booking-vs-install drift *against* this floor.
How much does a GPS fix jitter when the SAME home is sampled repeatedly?
That number is the floor -- no downstream drift analysis can claim structural
error below it (it is just apparatus noise).
Why wifi_connected_location_captured works as the apparatus:
Every Wiom router broadcasts a unique SSID (one per household). The Wiom app
emits this event every time it connects to that SSID -- first install AND every
later reinstall/reconnect. Same physical home, multiple GPS fixes across time.
Anchor on row_cnt=1 (first fix, SSID-validated at install).
Treat row_cnt>=2 as repeat samples of that same home.
Attrition (every step's freq sums back to raw):

column6: mobiles with only 1-2 pings drop -- no repeat measurement
subsequent pings only (anchor row excluded from pair count)
dropped pings within 15min of prev ping (cache/bug re-emits)
mobiles whose surviving pings fall <3 drop (all cache-hit bursts)
dropped subseq pings >250m from anchor (home move, not jitter)
mobiles with <2 surviving subseq pings drop -- need >=2 for dist stats

column1: Retention: 5.7% of raw mobiles. Most drop because they
emitted only 1 ping (no repeat measurement possible) -- structural, not a bug.
Per-ping jitter  = haversine(anchor lat/lng, subsequent ping lat/lng). ALWAYS vs the
row_cnt=1 anchor, NOT between consecutive pings. Consecutive-ping
distance would conflate sequential drift with apparatus noise.
Within any 15-min window for the same mobile, keep only the earliest ping.
Reason: Android GPS cache hits + known app re-emit bug produce duplicate fixes
on the same underlying reading. Not independent draws.
Impact at Stage A: 120,900 -> 60,661 pings (~50% are not independent samples).
Drop any subsequent ping > 250m from the anchor.
Reason: Delhi cohort has high rental turnover. >250m is almost certainly a home
change, not apparatus jitter. Keeping these would inflate the noise floor.
A mobile needs at least 2 subsequent pings to yield per-mobile min/max/mean/median
stats. Mobiles with only 1 subseq survive at the per-ping layer but drop from the
## 4. PINGS-PER-MOBILE (v4 COHORT)  -- confirms every mobile has >=3 total pings
74% of v4 mobiles have exactly 3 total pings (anchor + 2 subseq). Only ~9% have
5+ total pings. This shapes the GE5 sensitivity cohort in section 9.
Bucket: pd.qcut(distance_m, q=10). D1 = tightest, D10 = noisiest. freq sums to n.
D1-D8 (80% of pings) stay within 28m. D9 shoulders (28-89m). D10 carries the
full 89-250m tail -- ~10% of fixes are structurally bad (multipath, indoor, etc.).
Headline: p95 = 154.76m. THIS IS THE README'S STAGE B THRESHOLD.
Any booking-vs-install drift below 154.76m is indistinguishable from apparatus noise.
Definition: haversine(ping_{i-1}, ping_i) between TEMPORALLY ADJACENT pings
within each mobile's ordered timeline (anchor + surviving subseq pings).
Same cohort as anchor-based: v4 mobiles (8,317), same 15-min dedup + 250m cap.
Count: each subseq ping contributes exactly one consec-distance to its
predecessor -> 20,231 measurements, same as anchor-based count.
D10's max (320m) exceeds our 250m anchor cap -- geometrically valid: two pings
can each be within 250m of anchor but >250m from each other if on opposite sides.
## 6c. CONSECUTIVE vs ANCHOR-BASED  --  TEMPORAL CORRELATION IN GPS NOISE
Same v4 cohort, same count (20,231 pairs). Different reference frame:
anchor_m = haversine(row_cnt=1 fix, subseq fix)       (days to months apart)
consec_m = haversine(ping_{i-1}, ping_i, sorted time) (minutes to days apart)
Consecutive is UNIFORMLY tighter than anchor-based across every percentile.
The gap widens in the mid-tail:
Read: this is the signature of TEMPORAL CORRELATION in GPS noise. Two pings
minutes apart share recent conditions -- same satellite geometry, similar
multipath environment, same weather, same phone state. Over long anchor gaps
(days to months), those correlations break and errors compound independently.
1. The APPARATUS ITSELF is tight. Two independent readings taken close in time
land within 5.9m of each other (p50) -- the hardware is not the problem.
2. What PROPAGATES downstream is day-to-day drift, not instantaneous noise.
3. For Stage B (booking -> install is a days-apart comparison), anchor-based
p95 = 154.76m REMAINS the right subtraction floor, NOT consec p95 = 110.57m.
Booking-install is not a consecutive-reading comparison.
## 7. PER-MOBILE UNCERTAINTY RADIUS  --  max_dist_m DECILES (n = 8,317 mobiles)
Per mobile: max_dist_m = max(haversine(anchor, each subseq ping)).
Across mobiles: decile on max_dist_m. D1 = tightest radius, D10 = noisiest.
D1-D7 all end <=24m -- 70.0% of v4 mobiles (5,821) have their WORST fix within
25m of the anchor. This is the headline operational read.
D8 (23.67-46.14m, median 31.79m) straddles the 25m Promise Maker gate -- this is
the borderline population where the gate flips in/out based on which fix landed.
D10 (116-250m, median 176m) is the structural tail -- ~10% of mobiles.
Headline: p75(max_dist) = 31.79m -- for 75% of v4 mobiles (6,237), the worst
single fix is within ~32m of the true home. This is the operational bound we
can trust even on a bad individual reading.
CAVEAT: max_dist is an order statistic -- it grows with more pings per mobile.
Compare with GE5 cohort in section 9 to see the sampling effect.
## 9. PER-MOBILE CENTRAL TENDENCY  --  median_dist_m DECILES (n = 8,317 mobiles)
Per mobile: median_dist_m = median(haversine(anchor, subseq pings)).
More robust than max_dist to single outliers. Best signal for mobile segmentation.
p50 of median_dist (8.13m) ~ p50 per-ping (7.66m). Mobiles mostly do NOT have one
freak outlier -- they are either consistently clean OR consistently drifty. This
is a real Stage B handle: segment mobiles into 'clean' (low median) vs 'drifty'
## 10. SENSITIVITY  --  GE5 COHORT (mobiles with >=5 TOTAL pings)
GE5 = v4 mobiles with >=4 surviving subsequent pings (>=5 total incl. anchor).
Cohort: 736 mobiles / 3,664 subseq pings (8.8% of v4).
Purpose: per-mobile stats on 2 samples are noisy. GE5 gives tighter per-mobile
estimates at the cost of a much smaller, non-randomly-selected cohort.
a. Per-ping p95: 154.76m -> 119.95m (GE5 is tighter). The GE5 cohort is self-
selected for engagement (reinstalled 4+ times) -- likely cleaner users.
b. max_dist_m: p75 GROWS from 31.79 -> 37.11m. This is a sampling artifact,
NOT apparatus worsening -- max(N) grows with N. The 'within 25m' fraction
c. median_dist_m: p75 TIGHTENS from 20.66 -> 15.06m. More samples = more stable
per-mobile central estimate. This is the cleanest evidence that the APPARATUS
core is tight -- the noise in per-mobile estimates is sampling variance, not

column2: Stage A cohort tied exactly to Rohan's prior notebook run: 8,317 mobiles / 20,231 subseq pings, median radius 12m, p75 32m, p90 116m. Our re-pull is a clean replica.
Apparatus CORE is genuinely tight: per-ping p50 = 7.66m. Half of all independent GPS fixes of the same home land within 7.7m.
70.0% of mobiles (5,821 / 8,317) have their WORST single fix within 25m of the anchor. The 25m Promise Maker gate sits right at the D7/D8 boundary.
Per-ping p95 = 154.76m. This is the README Stage B noise-floor threshold: any booking-vs-install drift < 155m is indistinguishable from apparatus noise.
Mobile drift appears bimodal: per-mobile median closely tracks per-ping median (8m vs 7.7m). Mobiles are either CONSISTENTLY clean or CONSISTENTLY drifty -- not a few flukes per mobile.
D10 mobiles (~10% of v4) carry max_dist 116-250m. Even with the 250m cap, these represent structural capture error. Candidates for root-cause investigation (indoor, night, device type).

max_dist_m is an order statistic -- it inflates with more pings per mobile. GE5 cohort confirms median_dist tightens with more samples (20.7m -> 15.1m), while max_dist grows (31.8m -> 37.1m). Do NOT use max_dist trends across cohorts without normalizing for ping count.

15-min dedup dropped 50% of pings (120,900 -> 60,661). Android GPS cache hits + app re-emit bug are a first-order effect. Ignoring them inflates 0m-distance spikes and tightens the noise floor artificially.

Consecutive-ping jitter is UNIFORMLY tighter than anchor-based across every percentile (p50: 5.9 vs 7.7m, p75: 14.9 vs 20.0m, p95: 110.6 vs 154.8m). Signature of temporal correlation in GPS noise: temporally-adjacent fixes share recent conditions and their errors correlate; long-gap fixes don't.

Apparatus itself is tight: two independent readings of the same home minutes apart land within 5.9m of each other (consec p50). The downstream problem is NOT instantaneous apparatus noise -- it's day-to-day drift.

For Stage B, anchor-based p95 (154.8m) is the RIGHT subtraction floor, not consec p95 (110.6m). Booking-lat/lng and install-lat/lng are separated by days (fee capture -> scheduling -> install), so the relevant noise model is long-gap drift, not consecutive-reading precision.

column1: ## 12. OPEN QUESTIONS / NEXT STEPS (STAGE B HOOKS)

column3: GPS jitter baseline established -- p95 = 154.76m per-ping, p75 max_dist = 31.79m (n=8,317)
0m-spike diagnostic: what fraction of subseq pings are exactly 0m from anchor, and how does that % break down by time-gap bucket? Confirms cache vs stable-GPS.

Mobile bimodality segmentation: split v4 into 'clean' (median_dist <= 10m) and 'drifty' (median_dist > 50m). Carry that label into Stage B to see if drifty mobiles have systematically higher booking-vs-install drift.
Time-of-day effect (from Rohan's original hypothesis): do late-night/indoor pings have higher jitter? Join to fee_captured_at hour in Stage B.
Device self-report correlation: does booking_accuracy correlate with per-mobile median_dist? Rohan's notebook said 'WEAK' -- worth re-testing on the larger unified cohort in Stage B.
Stage B: compute install_drift_meters per installed booking. Subtract p95 jitter = 154.76m to get excess_drift_meters. Decile-slice and compare against Stage A distribution.

Stage B: % of installed bookings where install_drift > 25m. Every such booking is a lead where the 25m gate was applied to a lat/lng that was itself >25m off from where the customer actually lives -- a structural falsification of the gate's precondition.

Back-link to allocation_signal/: does the 448km D10 tail in nearest_distance correlate with mobiles that are in our D10 drift bucket here? That would confirm booking-lat/lng noise is inflating the allocation distance signal.
